Finding BBB-Permeable Molecules
that Target Pin1

Live agentic AI demo in biochemistry with Claude Code

Mike Hallett

Department of Biochemistry · Schulich School of Medicine & Dentistry

Western University

Pin1 & the Blood-Brain Barrier

Pin1 Biology

Pin1 — a prolyl isomerase, molecular switch post-phosphorylation.

Regulates tau tangles and APP processing (amyloid plaques).

Activators — not inhibitors — may be neuroprotective.

Opposite to cancer, where Pin1 is oncogenic.

The Blood-Brain Barrier

Brain's gatekeeper — tight endothelial layer controlling molecular entry.

Can't cross = drug never reaches target.

Crosses unexpectedly = dangerous side effects.

Predicting BBB+ vs BBB− from structure alone: key drug discovery challenge.

Our Challenge

Identify BBB+ molecules that can target Pin1.

A neuroprotective Pin1 activator must first cross the barrier to reach the brain.

Can agentic AI find candidates computationally, quickly?

The Data

B3DB Dataset — Meng et al. (2021)

~7,800 molecules compiled from 50 sources.

Labelled BBB+ or BBB−, encoded as SMILES strings.

Four reliability groups (A–D) based on evidence quality.

Provenance

Group A (1,058): numeric logBB from in vivo measurements.

Groups B/C (~6,700): categorical labels only — mix of experimental, clinical inference, and computational sources.

Evidence type not flagged per molecule.

Tools & Databases

RDKit — molecular descriptors
Streamlit — interactive exploration
fpocket — pocket detection
PubMed — literature
PubChem — compound data
ChEMBL — known ligands
ClinicalTrials.gov — CNS trials
ChemDB — molecule annotation
AlphaFold — protein structures

Claude Code — Overview

Why Claude Code for Biochemistry?

  • 📚 Easy access to literature & databases
  • 🔭 Peer FAR deeper into papers and associated data (GUIs on the fly)
  • 🔧 Installs its own tools
  • 📜 Writes awesome code (trust certificates — more later!)
  • 🔍 Transparent & auditable
  • 📊 Helps organize & prepare presentations
  • 🎓 Lowers the barrier for wet-lab scientists

The Agent Team

🎗 CONDUCTOR

Orchestration. Spawns and coordinates all subagents, relays messages between them, posts to Slack, assembles final report.

⚒ BLACKSMITH

Heavy lifting. Loads data, computes descriptors, trains classifiers, screens Pin1 candidates, builds interactive dashboard.

📚 BOOKWORM

Reads everything. Literature search, database queries (PubMed, PubChem, ChEMBL, ClinicalTrials.gov), summarises existing knowledge.

🎨 ARTIST

Visualisation. Figures, charts, 3D protein structures. Assembles polished PowerPoint.

⚔ ADVERSARY

Proves us wrong. Checks data leakage, demands cross-validation, questions evidence, flags risks. Built-in peer reviewer.

The Village

Conductor + four specialist agents running in parallel

It Takes a Village

Advantages of the multi-agent approach

⚡

Parallelism

Four agents working simultaneously. Nothing waits in line.

🎯

Specialisation

One job per agent, tuned tools and instructions. Four experts, not one generalist.

🔍

Built-in Scrutiny

Dedicated critic catches mistakes early, not after the fact.

💬

Natural Language Control

Plain English instructions. No code, no config, no CLI flags.

🔁

Reproducibility

All work logged, prompts saved, outputs versioned. Anyone can re-run.

🎓

Accessibility

No terminal experience needed. You bring the science, AI handles the code.

Under the hood

Anatomy of an agent definition — bookworm.md

Round 1 — Explore & Build

Getting the lay of the land

⚒ BLACKSMITH

Load B3DB (Meng et al.), compute RDKit descriptors, build Streamlit GUI. Show evidence provenance — experimental vs. computational — per molecule.

📚 BOOKWORM

Literature survey on Pin1 as CNS drug target — tau phosphorylation, APP processing, Alzheimer's, case for activators.

🎨 ARTIST

Dataset statistics: molecule counts, BBB+/BBB− balance, feature distributions.

⚔ ADVERSARY

Critique B3DB dataset provenance and methodology. Flag weaknesses; check if BBB+ molecules resemble known Pin1 ligands.

Round 1 — What we actually typed

Natural language → parallel agents

Round 2 — Screen & Validate

Hunting for Pin1 binders among BBB+ molecules

⚒ BLACKSMITH

Tanimoto similarity screen (~7,800 molecules) against known Pin1 ligands. Pharmacophore screening of BBB+ molecules against fpocket-identified binding pockets.

📚 BOOKWORM

ChEMBL: Pin1 inhibitors/activators. ClinicalTrials.gov: CNS trials. Key papers (Lu 2003, Pastorino 2006). Any known activators BBB+?

🎨 ARTIST

AlphaFold Pin1 structure coloured by pLDDT. Highlight WW & PPIase domains. Overlay candidate pharmacophores on binding pocket.

⚔ ADVERSARY

Binding evidence: convincing or speculative? AlphaFold confidence sufficient? Cancer tension: neuroprotective activator may be tumorigenic.

Round 3 — Synthesise & Present

Assembling the story

📚 BOOKWORM

Compile findings into PowerPoint: top BBB+ candidates, evidence, clinical trial status, Pin1 therapeutic hypothesis.

🎗 CONDUCTOR

Generate exam covering biology, computational methods, AI orchestration, and ethics.

Approaches with Agentic AI

Practice makes the difference

Naive Approach Mature Approach
Dynamic Prompting One giant prompt, expect perfection first try Iterative rounds — broad start, inspect, refine
Dedicated Oversight Agent marks its own homework Dedicated adversary audits every step
Trust through Certificates Accept output as ground truth Ask for evidence, ask for the logic Ctrl-O, or ask for the code
It takes a village One generalist, everything sequential Specialised parallel agents with tailored tools and constraints
AI & Me in Harmony AI replaces your expertise AI as force multiplier — you bring science, it brings compute
Controls Do I need to explain to this community?

Ask for Code, Not Answers

Two approaches — one scales

"Predict binding for these 1,000 molecules"

Claude does the work directly inside the conversation.

+ Fast, no programming needed

− Results vanish. Can't audit method. Doesn't scale. Not reproducible.

"Write a Python script that predicts binding for any set of molecules"

Claude writes the code — a script you run, inspect, modify, share.

+ Scales to millions. Reproducible. Persists. Code is a witness — every assumption visible and verifiable.

− Slower setup. Needs working environment. May need debugging.

The principle

Small & one-off → direct answer is fine.

Large, repeated, or scientific → ask for code. Code = product + proof.

Our demo: blacksmith writes real Python, not mental math.

Other citizens in my village

🔮
The Oracle
Cross-project memory keeper. Remembers genes, findings, and hypotheses across all lab repositories.
“Oracle, commit to memory that MMP11 is differentially expressed between DCIS and IDC in breast cancer.”
“Oracle, are any of the following genes X, Y, Z important in early breast lesions?”
⚖
The Conscience
EDID watchdog. Flags biases in experimental design, language, and literature—sex bias in mouse models, colonial metaphors, exclusionary framing.
“Conscience here, I object to the blacksmith referring to everyone as ‘boys’.”
“Conscience, in Biology of Cancer (Weinberg), identify all instances of colonial metaphors, sexist language, or other examples contrary to EDID goals.”
“Conscience here, this study used only male mice—results may not generalize across sexes.”
Saul Goodman — Lawyer focused on patent law. Reports on patent status of genes, gene products, molecules etc.
… and many others!
Robert Weinberg
Author, The Biology of Cancer

Additional Concepts

📜
Rules
Persistent instructions that shape agent behavior across sessions
⚡
Skills
Reusable prompt templates invoked as slash commands
🛡
Permissions
Fine-grained control over which tools agents can use
🔗
Hooks
Shell commands triggered by agent events (pre/post tool calls)

Issues & Ethics

rentahuman.ai

When AI needs hands

A marketplace where AI agents hire humans for physical-world tasks they can't do themselves.

Integrates via MCP server or REST API — agents search, book, and pay humans for meetings, errands, pickups, research, and more.
